events.title: First Known Valentine's Letter in English
events.summary: Margery Brews wrote the earliest surviving Valentine's letter in English.
events.details: In a letter to her fiancé John Paston, Margery called him her 'right well-beloved Valentine.' This charming historical document provides a glimpse into personal expressions of love from centuries ago.

events.title: U.S. Flag First Recognized by Foreign Power
events.summary: A foreign naval vessel gives the American flag its first salute.
events.details: The French ship Robuste fired a nine-gun salute to the American ship Ranger, commanded by John Paul Jones. This was the first official recognition of the American flag by a foreign government.

events.title: Oregon Joins the United States
events.summary: Oregon is officially admitted as the 33rd U.S. state.
events.details: President James Buchanan signed the bill admitting Oregon into the Union. This marked a significant milestone in American westward expansion and the development of the Pacific Northwest region.

events.title: Alexander Graham Bell Files Telephone Patent
events.summary: Bell filed his patent for the telephone, revolutionizing global communication.
events.details: On this day, Bell's lawyer filed the application for 'Improvements in Telegraphy.' This invention would fundamentally change how people connect and communicate across vast distances.

events.title: Oscar Wilde's Masterpiece Play Premieres
events.summary: 'The Importance of Being Earnest' by Oscar Wilde premiered in London.
events.details: This satirical comedy is considered a masterpiece of English literature. Its witty dialogue and clever social commentary have delighted audiences for over a century, cementing its classic status.

events.title: Arizona Becomes the 48th U.S. State
events.summary: Arizona is admitted to the Union as the 48th state.
events.details: President William Howard Taft signed the proclamation making Arizona the final of the contiguous states to be admitted. The state's entry completed the map of the lower 48 states.

events.title: League of Women Voters is Founded
events.summary: The League of Women Voters was established to help women vote.
events.details: Formed just six months before the 19th Amendment was ratified, the nonpartisan organization was created to help 20 million American women carry out their new responsibilities as voters.

events.title: The Birth of IBM Corporation
events.summary: The Computing-Tabulating-Recording Company was renamed International Business Machines (IBM).
events.details: Under Thomas J. Watson, this name change signaled the company's global ambition. IBM would become a leader in technological innovation for decades, shaping the modern world.

events.title: First General-Purpose Computer ENIAC Unveiled
events.summary: The Electronic Numerical Integrator and Computer (ENIAC) was formally dedicated.
events.details: This event marked the beginning of the computer age. ENIAC was a revolutionary machine that could solve a vast range of computing problems, paving the way for modern technology.

events.title: Australia Adopts Decimal Currency System
events.summary: Australia replaced pounds, shillings, and pence with dollars and cents.
events.details: This major national reform simplified financial transactions and aligned Australia's currency with global standards. An extensive public education campaign ensured a smooth transition for the country.

events.title: First GPS Satellite Placed into Orbit
events.summary: The first operational GPS satellite was launched, beginning an era of navigation.
events.details: This satellite, NAVSTAR 2-1, was the first of the Block II satellites that formed the modern GPS constellation. The system has since transformed travel, logistics, and daily life worldwide.

events.title: Voyager 1 Takes 'Pale Blue Dot' Photo
events.summary: The Voyager 1 space probe captured a historic image of Earth.
events.details: At Carl Sagan's request, NASA turned the probe's camera for one last look at Earth. This provided a powerful, humbling perspective on our planet's place in the universe.

events.title: First Spacecraft Orbits an Asteroid
events.summary: The NEAR Shoemaker probe successfully entered orbit around asteroid 433 Eros.
events.details: This was a major milestone in space exploration, providing unprecedented data about asteroids. It was the first mission dedicated to studying an asteroid up close for an extended period.

events.title: UNESCO Adopts Human Genetic Data Declaration
events.summary: UNESCO adopted a declaration to protect human rights regarding genetic data.
events.details: The International Declaration on Human Genetic Data balances scientific progress with fundamental human rights. It provides ethical principles for collecting, processing, using, and storing such sensitive data.

events.title: YouTube Video-Sharing Platform is Launched
events.summary: The YouTube domain was activated, launching the popular video-sharing service.
events.details: Founded by three former PayPal employees, YouTube revolutionized how people share and consume video. It has since become a global platform for entertainment, education, and communication.
